top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the inadequacy of traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls in managing AI agents, which are increasingly operating as highly privileged identities. The piece focuses on the security gap created by standing privilege models when applied to AI-driven autonomous agents, a direct challenge to current PAM architectures and access management practices.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total, including two zero-days. Elevation-of-privilege vulnerabilities are directly relevant to PAM and access control, as they represent attack vectors that can bypass authorization controls and grant unauthorized access.

top_stories.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
top_stories.summary: Specops outlines five methods by which a Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on credential theft and privilege escalation risks. The piece covers identity-first access controls, device trust enforcement, and lateral movement prevention as core Zero Trust principles.

top_stories.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
top_stories.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identities as a unified governance challenge in an interview with Help Net Security. He draws on experience from IBM and CyberArk to describe approaches to bringing visibility and governance to non-human identities.

top_stories.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
top_stories.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police have dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials and attempt over $20 million in fraud. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of detecting credential-based attacks, which increasingly blend in with normal business activity. It outlines fundamental shifts cybersecurity teams need to make in their detection models to counter identity-based threats.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group is continuing a global cyber espionage campaign targeting organizations of varying technical sophistication. Security experts recommend patching and zero trust implementation as non-negotiable defensive measures against the threat group.

news.title: Anthropic tests user trust with ID and selfie checks for Claude
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced identity verification for its Claude AI chatbot, requiring users to submit government-issued ID and selfie checks, marking the first major AI chatbot to implement such a requirement. The verification process involves document and biometric checks, positioning it adjacent to IAM concerns around identity proofing and digital identity verification workflows.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to prevent session token theft. DBSC binds authenticated sessions to a specific device using cryptographic keys, making stolen session cookies unusable on other devices.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user authentication at scale.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" bypasses traditional phishing defenses by proxying real login pages and acting as a man-in-the-middle relay between victims and legitimate sites. The service captures usernames, passwords, and MFA codes in real-time by forwarding credentials to the actual site and returning responses, effectively defeating MFA protections.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring MFA capabilities to web-based applications. Limited details are available from the description, but the product targets multi-factor authentication delivery via browser extension.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity capabilities as part of a broader AI infrastructure strategy. The acquisition aims to strengthen Cisco's identity security offerings in the context of agentic AI, positioning identity as a control plane for AI-driven environments.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is a new open-source tool (Apache 2.0, written in Rust) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the Linux host via PAM. It differentiates itself from other OIDC-for-SSH solutions by implementing DPoP (RFC 9449), which binds tokens cryptographically to the requester's private key to prevent token replay attacks.

news.title: Identity Protection in the AI Era
news.summary: The article presents a framework for identity-first security that incorporates visibility, threat detection, zero trust enforcement, AI protection, and threat intelligence into a unified model. It addresses the intersection of AI and identity protection, covering key IAM concepts including zero trust enforcement and threat response.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-backed keys to bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, addressing a significant session hijacking threat relevant to identity and access management.

news.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
news.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting corporate breach campaigns by impersonating support staff and using fake Okta login pages to steal credentials and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems. The campaign combines social engineering with phishing infrastructure designed to mimic Okta authentication pages, enabling data theft from targeted organizations.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks, where infostealer malware extracts and exfiltrates authentication cookies. The feature cryptographically binds session credentials to the device, preventing stolen cookies from being used on attacker-controlled systems.
